results.input: Hi! I want to order a cappuccino and a cheese sandwich.
results.response_preview: That is a great start, but in a coffee shop, we often use 'I would like' to sound more polite. Instead of 'I want', say 'I would like a cappuccino' (Eu gostaria de um cappuccino).

results.input: Can I have it to go, please?
results.response_preview: Sure thing, I'll put that in a to-go cup for you. Do you want your cheese sandwich toasted or cold?

results.input: I need to check in for my flight to New York.
results.response_preview: I can certainly help you with your flight to New York. May I please have your passport and booking reference?

results.input: My gate changed? I am not sure where to go.
results.response_preview: Don't worry, gates often change at big airports like this one. You can say: 'Where is the new gate?' (Onde fica o novo portão?) to ask for directions. Where are you flying today?

results.input: I have a headache and sore throat since yesterday.
results.response_preview: I'm sorry to hear that you're feeling unwell. On a scale of one to ten, how bad is the pain in your throat?

results.input: Should I take this medicine before or after meals?
results.response_preview: That is a very important question to ask! Usually, for this type of medicine, you should take it 'after meals' (depois das refeições) so it doesn't hurt your stomach. What symptoms

results.input: I want to open a checking account.
results.response_preview: I can certainly help you open a checking account today. May I please see your ID and your proof of address?

results.input: What documents do I need to rent a car?
results.response_preview: I'll need your driver's license and a credit card in your name. Do you have a reservation or would you like to rent a car now?

results.input: Could you show me where the fitting room is?
results.response_preview: Of course, the fitting rooms are right behind those racks! Before you go, you can also ask: 'Do you have this in a different color?' (Você tem isso em uma cor diferente?). What siz

results.input: I think these shoes are too expensive. Any discount?
results.response_preview: That is a good question, but in a store, it is more natural to ask: 'Is there a discount on these?' (Tem algum desconto nestes?). To be even more polite, you can say: 'Is this the

results.input: I has two brother and they is very funny.
results.response_preview: That sounds like a lively house! Instead of 'they is', say: 'they are'. I am an only child, so I'm curious. Are they older or younger than you?

results.input: Yesterday I go to the market and buyed apples.
results.response_preview: Oh, nice! Instead of 'go' and 'buyed', say: went and bought. I went to the market too and I found some fresh bread. What did you make with the apples?

results.input: She don't like spicy food, but she like pasta.
results.response_preview: My sister is the same! Instead of 'she don't like', say: 'she doesn't like'. Does she cook pasta herself, or does she eat at restaurants?

results.input: Can you explain when to use some and any?
results.response_preview: I have some free time now! I use 'some' for positive things and 'any' for questions. Do you have any plans for dinner tonight?

results.input: If I will have time, I will visit my grandma.
results.response_preview: That's so kind! Instead of 'will have time', say: 'have time'. If I have time, I always call my family. If you visit her, will you bring a gift?

results.input: I have been to London two times since 2020.
results.response_preview: That's cool! I've never been to London. Have you seen the Big Ben clock tower while visiting there?

results.input: The cake was make by my sister.
results.response_preview: Instead of <make by>, say: made by. Oh, I love cake! Was it made for a special party or just for fun?

results.input: He is more taller than me.
results.response_preview: Instead of <more taller>, say: taller. I am taller than my sister, but she is the smartest. Is your friend taller or shorter than you?

results.input: Can you help me prepare for a job interview?
results.response_preview: I would love to help you practice for your interview! A great way to start is by introducing yourself. Why do you want this job?

results.input: What strengths should I mention for a customer support role?
results.response_preview: Hello, I'm Sarah Mitchell. For this Junior Analyst role, you should highlight your problem-solving and communication skills; could you start by telling me about your background and

results.input: I lost my wallet in the train station.
results.response_preview: I'm sorry to hear that you lost your wallet. Around what time and where exactly in the station did you last have it?

results.input: How can I ask for directions politely in English?
results.response_preview: That is a very useful thing to learn! To be polite, you can start with: 'Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to...?' (Com licença, você poderia me dizer como chegar a...?). Can

results.input: Can I book a room for two nights with breakfast included?
results.response_preview: Welcome to The Grand View Hotel. May I have your name to check for a reservation?

results.input: I need to reschedule my dentist appointment.
results.response_preview: I'm sorry you have to change your plans! Instead of 'in the train station', it's better to say: 'at the train station' (na estação de trem). What brings you in today?

results.input: Could you recommend a beginner workout plan?
results.response_preview: I can definitely help you with a beginner plan! Instead of 'the Big Ben', we usually just say 'Big Ben' (o Big Ben). What kind of workout do you want today?

results.input: I need to mail this package internationally.
results.response_preview: I can help you with that international mailing. Which country are you sending this package to?

results.input: Can you suggest a nice conversation opener for a first date?
results.response_preview: That is an excellent question! A great way to start is: 'I've been looking forward to meeting you' (Eu estava ansioso(a) para te conhecer). Can you tell me a bit more about that?

results.input: I want to practice small talk with my neighbors.
results.response_preview: Hey there! I was just grabbing the mail and noticed how nice your lawn looks.

results.input: Please correct this sentence: 'She have went to school'.
results.response_preview: Instead of 'She have went', say: 'She has gone to school'. To talk about where someone is right now, you can say: 'She is at school' (Ela está na escola). Can you tell me a bit mor

results.input: Let's finish: give me one realistic phrase I can use today at work.
results.response_preview: That is a great goal for today! A very professional and realistic phrase is: 'Could you please give me an update on this project?' (Você poderia, por favor, me dar uma atualização
